reasoning: Key evidence synthesis: 1) Tesla was excluded from BC Hydro EV charger rebates on March 12, 2025 as political retaliation for US trade measures (search_0_result_0, search_0_result_6). 2) This is tied to broader Canada-US trade disputes around Columbia River Treaty talks (search_1_summary). 3) BC's main passenger vehicle EV rebate program is pausing May 15, 2025, but home charger programs may differ. However, the critical ambiguity is whether the specific home charger rebate program itself will still exist in July 2025. The 'pause' on passenger vehicle rebates creates uncertainty. 4) The timeline is very short (1.5 months from knowledge cutoff to resolution), making it unlikely dramatic policy reversals occur unless underlying trade issues resolve, which they appear not to have. 5) Ultimate probability and confidence balanced: 73% YES that Tesla remains excluded and 27% NO that program ends completely. Confidence is medium due to remaining ambiguity about program continuity.
